title: Counting the cost of heat: the case for urgent solutions for cities
content: This report reveals that extreme heat is emerging as one of the most underestimated threats to economic development worldwide, hammering city economies, overwhelming health systems, and falling hardest on women working on the frontlines of a hotter world.

title: Extreme heat costing poor women workers over $57 billion in earnings a year
content: A landmark global economic analysis, paired with a state-of-the-art heat solution cost-benefit calculator covering 11,408 cities worldwide, shows that investing in heat action saves billions in losses.

title: Heat stress exposure climbed from 16% to 22% worldwide over 50 years, study shows
content: The number of people exposed to dangerous heat stress worldwide has risen sharply over the past half-century, propelled by climate change, according to a study released Monday as Europe sweltered through a punishing heat wave.

title: Handbook on macroeconomic modelling for climate resilience
content: This handbook provides guidance for designing and implementing technical assistance on macroeconomic modelling to support climate resilient economic development in partner countries.
snippet: This handbook provides guidance for designing and implementing technical assistance on macroeconomic modelling to support climate resilient economic development in partner countries.

title: Public encouraged to ‘cool off in culture’ as second heatwave of the year hits and health alerts issued
content: The BFI, Arts Council England, LIVE Green, and Julie’s Bicycle have partnered to roll out Cool Off in Culture, a UK-wide campaign signposting the public to cultural venues during increasingly frequent heatwaves.
snippet: The BFI, Arts Council England, LIVE Green, and Julie’s Bicycle have partnered to roll out Cool Off in Culture, a UK-wide campaign signposting the public to cultural venues during increasingly frequent

title: British Film Institute (BFI)
content: The British Film Institute is a film and television charitable organisation which promotes and preserves independent and mainstream filmmaking and television in the United Kingdom.
snippet: The British Film Institute is a film and television charitable organisation which promotes and preserves independent and mainstream filmmaking and television in the United Kingdom.

title: Navigating the Indian power sector in the face of extreme heat: A scoping note
content: This scoping note examines how intensifying heat-waves threaten every link of India’s power-sector value chain and outlines a roadmap for climate-ready electricity systems.
snippet: This scoping note examines how intensifying heat-waves threaten every link of India’s power-sector value chain and outlines a roadmap for climate-ready electricity systems.

title: Heat smart schools
content: Heat Smart Schools is a guidance framework by CDRI to help schools adapt to rising extreme heat risks. With over two billion children projected to face frequent heatwaves by 2050, the document emphasizes that heat threatens health, learning, and equity.

title: High-severity fires burn 30 times more acreage than 40 years ago, researchers find
content: Forest fires now burn ten times more acreage annually than in 1985, while wildfire severity has gotten even worse. In California, 30 times more acreage burned from high-severity, forest-killing fires, according to new UCLA research.

title: El Niño is coming. Here is where the risks to agriculture are highest
content: FAO experts map where crops and pasturelands are most vulnerable to drought.
snippet: FAO experts map where crops and pasturelands are most vulnerable to drought.

title: Understanding the global landscape of heat early warning systems
content: This report provides a global overview Heat Early Warning Systems, examining where they exist, how they are designed and operated, and their challenges and opportunities.
snippet: This report provides a global overview Heat Early Warning Systems, examining where they exist, how they are designed and operated, and their challenges and opportunities.

title: HERA launches the world’s first heat-pregnancy insurance to protect expectant mothers from extreme heat
content: HERA and its partners launched HERA Materna, the world’s first ever heat-pregnancy insurance program. Extreme heat is threatening pregnant women on the frontlines of the climate crisis, and until now no financial protection has existed to shield them.

title: Increase in wildfire-driven ozone linked to premature deaths across the U.S.
content: Smog from wildfires is getting worse across much of the U.S., according to a NASA-funded study.
snippet: Smog from wildfires is getting worse across much of the U.S., according to a NASA-funded study.

title: Heat waves can leave homes dangerously hot – even for young, healthy adults
content: Most people know that heat waves can be dangerous. What they may not realize is that the heat indoors can be much worse than outdoors.
snippet: Most people know that heat waves can be dangerous. What they may not realize is that the heat indoors can be much worse than outdoors.

title: Climate action as peace enabler: lessons from the Sahel on delivering peace and social dividends in fragile settings
content: This case study examines the Justice and Stability in the Sahel (JASS) programme in Mali and Niger to explore whether climate action in the Sahel can generate broader peace and social benefits alongside enhanced resilience.
snippet: This case study examines the Justice and Stability in the Sahel (JASS) programme in Mali and Niger to explore whether climate action in the Sahel can generate broader peace and social benefits

title: Record-breaking heat spreads through Europe
content: An extraordinary heatwave in Europe has shattered numerous temperature records and had major impacts on human health, ecosystems, agriculture, infrastructure and labour productivity.
snippet: An extraordinary heatwave in Europe has shattered numerous temperature records and had major impacts on human health, ecosystems, agriculture, infrastructure and labour productivity.

title: Across Europe, heat adaptation plans are being put to a brutal test
content: Cities across the world are still preparing for the heat that we're experiencing today.
snippet: Cities across the world are still preparing for the heat that we're experiencing today.

title: As communities face more frequent hazard warnings, we need better systems to avoid ‘emergency fatigue’

snippet: Earlier this month, Wellington declared a local state of emergency, including evacuation orders, when forecast powerful swells threatened to inundate coastal properties.

title: Venezuela’s deadly earthquakes happened on a fault similar to the San Andreas, and the risks aren’t over yet – a geophysicist explains
content: University of Southern California geophysicist Sylvain Barbot explained what’s known about the earthquake pulses so far, what risks are still ahead and why Californians should pay attention.
snippet: University of Southern California geophysicist Sylvain Barbot explained what’s known about the earthquake pulses so far, what risks are still ahead and why Californians should pay attention.

title: Updated Return on Investment for preparedness to respond study
content: This report presents updated findings from WFP’s Return on Investment (ROI) for Preparedness study, demonstrating the value of pre-shock investments in strengthening emergency response.
snippet: This report presents updated findings from WFP’s Return on Investment (ROI) for Preparedness study, demonstrating the value of pre-shock investments in strengthening emergency response.

title: Powerful solar storms may change weather patterns across North America

title: ‘A sad inevitability’: after decades of climate warnings, why is Europe so unprepared for rising heat?
content: Heatwaves still bring large parts of Europe to their knees.
snippet: Heatwaves still bring large parts of Europe to their knees.

title: Heatwaves, air conditioning, and blackouts: Quantifying the real benefits of rooftop solar energy
content: Air conditioning is increasingly the go-to when heatwaves strike. However, not only does this lead to more emissions, it also puts increasing strain on the electricity grid – even culminating in deadly blackouts.

title: Study tackles water insecurity in Pune, India—challenges and policy solutions
content: Rapidly growing cities face water security challenges due to rising demand and limited freshwater supplies.
snippet: Rapidly growing cities face water security challenges due to rising demand and limited freshwater supplies.

title: Europe is battling a record‑breaking heatwave. What’s making it so severe?
content: Sweltering temperatures are shattering records across Europe, as the continent battles a deadly heatwave.
snippet: Sweltering temperatures are shattering records across Europe, as the continent battles a deadly heatwave.

title: Summer’s new normal is a hazard that’s testing Europe’s climate resilience
content: It is only June, and Europe is already baking through its second extreme heatwave in two months. Temperatures have topped 44 degrees Celsius in parts of the continent.
snippet: It is only June, and Europe is already baking through its second extreme heatwave in two months. Temperatures have topped 44 degrees Celsius in parts of the continent.

title: Fossil fuel emissions have rapidly worsened European heatwaves in just a few decades
content: Just weeks after a severe heatwave that broke all-time May records, Europe is experiencing another major heatwave that is breaking June and annual records.
snippet: Just weeks after a severe heatwave that broke all-time May records, Europe is experiencing another major heatwave that is breaking June and annual records.

title: Flood risk and social deprivation

title: Extreme heat is muddling animals’ brains—and even triggering aggression
content: When it’s hot outside, birds struggle to learn, dogs bite more often, goat-like chamois pick fights.
snippet: When it’s hot outside, birds struggle to learn, dogs bite more often, goat-like chamois pick fights.

title: Weather Jiu-Jitsu: Prospects for atmospheric nudging to reduce catastrophic weather extremes
content: This essay explores a novel approach to reducing the impacts of extreme weather through small, strategically timed atmospheric interventions guided by advanced forecasting.
snippet: This essay explores a novel approach to reducing the impacts of extreme weather through small, strategically timed atmospheric interventions guided by advanced forecasting.

title: The case for hiring a Chief Resilience Officer
content: When disruption strikes a modern enterprise, the greatest damage often comes not from the initial event but from how failures spread through connected systems.
snippet: When disruption strikes a modern enterprise, the greatest damage often comes not from the initial event but from how failures spread through connected systems.

title: Protecting value in at-risk developments: The case for property flood resilience (PFR)

title: Public First
content: Public First is a policy, research, opinion and strategy consultancy. They work with global companies, governments, start-ups, institutions and foundations around the world to tackle major public policy and strategic challenges.

title: Preparing for El Niño: Keeping teachers and learners safe during climate disasters

snippet: This is a webinar on preparing education systems for natural and climate-induced hazards related to this year’s El Niño weather system.

title: Policy brief: Strengthening local government action on urban heat in Sri Lankan cities: UHI-City resilience scorecard & comprehensive guidance note
content: This study seeks to understand how UHI risks are perceived, prioritised, and addressed in Sri Lankan cities, identify barriers and enablers influencing local government action.
snippet: This study seeks to understand how UHI risks are perceived, prioritised, and addressed in Sri Lankan cities, identify barriers and enablers influencing local government action.

title: Harnessing NISAR: Next-generation radar observations for earth applications

title: How hazards turn into disasters: Perspectives of emergency responders
content: This study investigates how disaster and emergency management professionals prioritize the factors that shape disaster risk and resilience across different contexts.
snippet: This study investigates how disaster and emergency management professionals prioritize the factors that shape disaster risk and resilience across different contexts.

title: Was Venezuela struck by an earthquake ‘doublet’? Here’s what we know so far
content: The doublets in Venezuela occurred along the diffuse onshore boundary between the Caribbean and South American tectonic plates.
snippet: The doublets in Venezuela occurred along the diffuse onshore boundary between the Caribbean and South American tectonic plates.

title: Expanding the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction to include animals
content: Policy paper proposing an animal-inclusive approach to the Sendai Framework by integrating animal welfare, biodiversity and sentient beings into disaster risk reduction.
snippet: Policy paper proposing an animal-inclusive approach to the Sendai Framework by integrating animal welfare, biodiversity and sentient beings into disaster risk reduction.

title: Europe’s extreme heat is shutting down power plants
content: Rising temperatures can affect our power supply, including nuclear and natural-gas power plants
snippet: Rising temperatures can affect our power supply, including nuclear and natural-gas power plants

title: Making Delhi Heat-Resilient: A roadmap with the focus on vulnerable groups
content: The study aims to assess Delhi's increasing heat stress and looks into propose roadmap that combines year-round city-wide heat resilience measures with targeted interventions for vulnerable groups.
snippet: The study aims to assess Delhi's increasing heat stress and looks into propose roadmap that combines year-round city-wide heat resilience measures with targeted interventions for vulnerable groups.
